title: AI Guardian Tray Plugin
description: Plugin definition for the AI Guardian system tray menu. Each JSON file in the tray-plugins directory defines one submenu with a name and a list of menu items.

$schema.description: JSON Schema reference for IDE autocompletion

name.description: Display name of the plugin submenu in the system tray

scope.description: Where this plugin appears. 'daemon' (default) shows inside daemon submenus filtered by tags. 'global' shows at the tray top level, not inside any daemon submenu.

tags.description: Tags to filter this plugin to specific daemons. Only shown on daemons with at least one matching menu_tags entry. Omit or leave empty to show on all daemons.

items.description: Menu items in this plugin (max 12 per plugin due to pre-allocated tray slots)

plugin_item.description: A single menu item that runs a command when clicked

label.description: Text displayed in the tray menu for this item

command.description: Command to execute. Either a plain string or a platform map object with keys like 'darwin', 'linux', 'windows', 'default'. Use {tray.param_name} for user parameter placeholders and {variable} for target variable placeholders (container_id, host, port, etc.).

type.description: Execution type: 'terminal' opens a terminal window, 'background' runs silently, 'notification' shows output as a system notification, 'clipboard' copies output to clipboard, 'modal' shows output in a dialog box

run_on_target.description: If true, wrap the command for execution on the daemon's target runtime (container exec, kubectl exec). Only applies when the daemon monitors a container or Kubernetes pod.

target.description: Multi-target execution mode. 'select' shows an interactive target picker at execution time. 'all' runs on all discovered targets without prompt. 'containers' runs on all container targets without prompt. Omit for default single-target behavior.

params.description: User-defined parameters that prompt for input before running. Values are substituted into the command via {tray.param_name} placeholders.

platform_command.description: Platform-specific command map. The command matching the current OS is used, falling back to 'default' if no platform match.

default.description: Fallback command when no platform-specific command matches

plugin_param.description: Parameter definition that prompts the user for input before running the command

hint.description: Help text shown to the user when prompting for this parameter

default.description: Default value pre-filled in the input prompt

options.description: Predefined choices shown as a dropdown instead of free-text input

type.description: Field type controlling the widget and validation. 'string' (default) shows a text input, 'int'/'number' validates numeric input, 'boolean' shows a checkbox, 'choice' shows a dropdown, 'combobox' shows an editable input with suggestions.

required.description: Whether the field must have a value before submission. Defaults to true.

pattern.description: Regex pattern to validate string input (e.g. '^[a-z]+$'). Only applies to 'string' type.

min.description: Minimum value for 'int' or 'number' type parameters.

max.description: Maximum value for 'int' or 'number' type parameters.
